en: Not many of you should become teachers, my brothers, for you know that we who teach will be judged with greater strictness.
zh: 我的弟兄們，你們不應該有太多人作教師，因為知道我們作教師的將受更嚴厲的審判。

en: For we all stumble in many ways, and if anyone does not stumble in what he says, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle his whole body.
zh: 我們在許多的事上都有過錯，假如有人在言語上沒有過錯，他就是完全的人，也能夠控制全身。

en: If we put bits into the mouths of horses so that they obey us, we guide their whole bodies as well.
zh: 我們若把嚼環扣入馬嘴，使牠們馴服，就能駕馭牠們的全身。

en: Look at the ships also: though they are so large and are driven by strong winds, they are guided by a very small rudder wherever the will of the pilot directs.
zh: 試看，船隻雖然甚大，又被狂風催逼，舵手只用小小的舵，就可以隨意操縱。

en: So also the tongue is a small member, yet it boasts of great things. How great a forest is set ablaze by such a small fire!
zh: 照樣，舌頭雖然是個小肢體，卻會說誇大的話。 試看，星星之火，可以燎原；

en: And the tongue is a fire, a world of unrighteousness. The tongue is set among our members, staining the whole body, setting on fire the entire course of life, and set on fire by hell.
zh: 舌頭就是火，在我們百體中，是個不義的世界，能污穢全身，把整個生命在運轉中焚燒起來，而且是被地獄之火點燃的。

en: For every kind of beast and bird, of reptile and sea creature, can be tamed and has been tamed by mankind,
zh: 各類飛禽、走獸、昆蟲、水族，都可以馴服，而且都已經被人類制伏了；

en: but no human being can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
zh: 可是沒有人能夠制伏舌頭；它是喋喋不休的惡物，充滿了致命的毒素。

en: With it we bless our Lord and Father, and with it we curse people who are made in the likeness of God.
zh: 我們用它來稱頌我們的主和天父，又用它來咒詛照 神的形象被造的人。

en: From the same mouth come blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to be so.
zh: 同一張嘴竟然又稱頌主，又咒詛人；我的弟兄們，這是不應該的！

en: Does a spring pour forth from the same opening both fresh and salt water?
zh: 同一泉眼裡能夠湧出甜水和苦水來嗎？

en: Can a fig tree, my brothers, bear olives, or a grapevine produce figs? Neither can a salt pond yield fresh water.
zh: 我的弟兄們，無花果樹能結橄欖嗎？葡萄樹能長無花果嗎？鹹水也不能發出甜水來。

en: Who is wise and understanding among you? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom.
zh: 你們中間誰是有智慧、有見識的呢？他就應當有美好的生活，用明智的溫柔，把自己的行為表現出來。

en: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast and be false to the truth.
zh: 如果你們心中存著刻薄的嫉妒和自私，就不可誇口，也不可說謊抵擋真理。

en: This is not the wisdom that comes down from above, but is earthly, unspiritual, demonic.
zh: 這種智慧不是從天上來的，而是屬地的、屬血氣的和屬鬼魔的。

en: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there will be disorder and every vile practice.
zh: 因為凡有嫉妒和自私的地方，就必有擾亂和各樣的壞事。

en: But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, open to reason, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial and sincere.
zh: 至於從天上來的智慧，首先是純潔的，其次是和平的，溫柔的，謙遜的，滿有恩慈和善果，沒有偏袒，沒有虛偽。

en: And a harvest of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
zh: 這是締造和平的人，用和平所培植出來的義果。
